page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY processor. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It features no local storage, requiring external network-attached storage, and does not include any GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile characterized by moderate multi-threaded capabilities alongside weaker single-core execution. In single-core tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, the instance scores in the bottom 25% tier, with single-threaded gzip compression falling into the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-threaded benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core, stress-ng multi-core, and bzip3 decompression, consistently register in the middle 50% average tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also align with average performance tiers, as do Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.se1ne.8xlarge offers a high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require top-tier single-threaded CPU performance. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core processing speed relative to newer processor generations, which may bottleneck serial tasks. This resource profile is appropriate for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, multi-threaded data processing, and moderate web serving applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.8xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz on a KVM hypervisor. Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, it provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench, while multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently achieve average mid-tier results. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that benefit from parallel execution rather than high single-threaded clock speeds. It is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core and weak single-core performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.8xlarge features 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86 memory-optimized instance featuring high RAM density and dedicated multi-core processing for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs and 256 GB of system memory.
High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
Weak single-core processing speeds and no local storage.
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and multi-threaded workloads.
